headlines.text: Trump extends Iran deadline again — gives Tehran until April 6 to open Strait of Hormuz or face energy strikes
Senate passes DHS funding bill at 2am, excluding ICE and Border Patrol — heads to House with uncertain future
Iran's Red Crescent says death toll in Iran now nears 2,000 as US and Israeli strikes intensify
Brent crude tops $110/barrel as Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed for nearly four weeks
Massive 'No Kings' protests planned for tomorrow — organizers say March 28 could be largest single-day protest in US history
S&P 500 logs fifth straight week of losses — worst losing streak since Russia invaded Ukraine in 2022
Iran's IRGC navy commander killed in Israeli airstrike, Israel vows to 'intensify and expand' attacks
Project Hail Mary breaks records with $80.5M opening weekend — Ryan Gosling delivers biggest non-franchise debut since Oppenheimer
Philippines declares state of emergency over energy crisis as diesel and petrol prices more than double since Iran war began
BTS drops comeback album 'Arirang' and free Seoul concert after military service hiatus — K-pop chaos resumes

breaking.text: BREAKING: Israel strikes Iran's Arak nuclear facility Friday evening as IDF issues evacuation warnings to nearby civilians

stories.hed: Trump Gives Iran 10 More Days — The Dealmaker Who Doesn't Want a Deal
stories.dek: Trump extended his Strait of Hormuz deadline to April 6, saying talks are going 'very well' while Iran says they are 'not negotiating.' Somewhere in the middle, Pakistan is playing telephone between two countries who both claim not to be talking. The IRGC navy chief is dead, Israel vows to escalate, and Iran just started charging ships $2 million each to maybe not get blown up in the world's most expensive toll lane.

stories.hed: Philippines Declares Energy Emergency as Iran War Doubles Fuel Prices Overnight
stories.dek: The Philippines became the first country to declare a state of emergency due to the global energy crisis, with diesel and petrol prices more than doubling since the US-Israel war on Iran began. Hundreds marched through Manila on Friday protesting soaring fuel costs, a preview of what economists warn could spread to dozens of import-dependent nations.

stories.hed: Senate Passes DHS Funding at 2am — TSA Workers May Finally Get Paid After 42 Days
stories.dek: In a voice vote at 2:20am, the Senate unanimously passed a bill to fund most of DHS — including the TSA and Coast Guard — while leaving out ICE and Border Patrol funding that Democrats refused to support. The bill now heads to a House Freedom Caucus that has already called it 'bad' and 'offensive,' so buckle up.

stories.hed: 'No Kings' Protests Set for Tomorrow — Could Be Largest Single-Day Demonstration in US History
stories.dek: Over 3,000 protests organized by Indivisible and AFL-CIO are planned across all 50 states for Saturday, March 28, fueled by ICE killings, the Iran war, and the DHS shutdown. Organizers expect it to dwarf the 7 million who turned out in October 2025, and cities from Philadelphia to LA are already closing streets.

stories.hed: Oil Trading Surged Minutes Before Trump Announced 'Productive' Iran Talks — Coincidence Is Working Hard Today
stories.dek: Multiple reports flagged a suspicious spike in oil trading activity in the minutes before Trump posted his Truth Social announcement claiming Iran talks were going well — a pattern observers noted has occurred more than once during the war. Markets have whipsawed on every Trump diplomatic signal, creating an unusually lucrative environment if you happened to know what was coming.

stories.hed: CPAC Opens in Texas With a Notably Anxious Vibe — Trump Isn't Even Showing Up
stories.dek: Day one of the Conservative Political Action Conference in Grapevine, Texas featured wall-to-wall Trump praise, but attendees told reporters the MAGA movement is quietly fractured over the Iran war, Epstein files, and a growing enthusiasm gap with Democrats. Trump skipped the event for the first time since 2016.

markets.context: Markets logged their fifth straight week of losses — worst streak since Russia invaded Ukraine — as the Strait of Hormuz stayed shut, Brent crude topped $110, and China opened a trade probe against the US, making stagflation feel less hypothetical by the hour.

gas.note: Gas is now $1 more than it was a month ago, which means you are paying Iran's war budget one tank at a time.

popCulture.hed: Project Hail Mary Launches Ryan Gosling Into the Stratosphere With $80.5M Opening — Biggest Non-Franchise Debut Since Oppenheimer
popCulture.dek: The Ryan Gosling sci-fi epic adapted from Andy Weir's novel opened to $80.5 million domestically, a record for Amazon MGM and the best non-franchise opening since Oppenheimer. Directed by the Lego Movie duo Phil Lord and Christopher Miller, 83% of audiences said they'd 'definitely recommend' it — which in 2026 is essentially a presidential approval rating most people can only dream of.

genZ.definition: A viral TikTok filter and meme character from the Italian brainrot craze — an unsettling anthropomorphic wooden plank from Indonesian culture that randomly intrudes on normal activities like going to the gym or finishing an assignment, backed by a creepy repetitive drumbeat. It's funny because the sheer randomness of a haunted plank showing up during mundane tasks perfectly captures the feeling of 2026.
genZ.usage: Me trying to negotiate the price of gas down to $3 when Tung Tung Tung Sahur shows up in the Strait of Hormuz and starts charging $2 million for safe passage.

tldr: Day 28 of the Iran war: Trump extended his Strait of Hormuz deadline again, Israel struck a nuclear facility, oil hit $110, markets had their worst week since Russia invaded Ukraine, and the Senate passed a DHS funding bill at 2am that the House Freedom Caucus promptly called offensive — all while 3,000 'No Kings' protests are loading up for tomorrow. Perfectly normal Friday.
